[et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" custom_css_free_form="selector textarea.answer {|| width: 100%;|| height: 250px;||}||||selector button.submit {|| width: 100px;|| max-width: 100px;|| height:50px;|| text-align: center;||}||||selector label.answer {|| font-size: 22px;||}||||selector .important {|| font-weight: bolder;||}||||selector textarea.existing-answer {|| display:none;||}||||div.status {|| display: flex;|| justify-content: space-between;|| align-items: center;||}||||p.left {|| flex: 1;|| text-align: left;||}||||p.middle {|| flex: 2;|| text-align: center;||}||||button.right {|| flex: 1;|| text-align: right;||}||||selector span.progress {|| color: grey;||}||||selector div#buttons {|| display: flex;|| flex-direction: column;|| align-items: flex-end; /* Align to the right */||}||||selector div#buttons button {|| margin-bottom: 10px; /* Adds spacing between buttons */||}||||/* span.group-completed::before {|| content: %22%92f00c%22; || font-family: 'Font Awesome 5 Free';|| font-weight: 900;||}||span.group-pending::before {||} */||||/* selector .elegant_icon {|| font: ||} */" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row admin_label="Header" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Title" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"]

Questions for Conference 2026

[header]

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Information" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Deadline" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][days_remaining extend_by="4" end_date="2026-02-07 12:00"] Note: If all answers not filled before the deadline the drafts will be used.[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_3,1_3,1_3" admin_label="Heads up" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" sticky_enabled="0"][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Progress" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_css_free_form="selector div#progress table td.value { || padding: 0px 0px 0px 6px;||}||" global_colors_info="{}"][progress_table][/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Status" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"]

[status position=top]

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Control" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][control position=top][/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Banner" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Message " _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"]

Please consider the contribution each question has in carrying the message, together with any financial and practical implications.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 1" module_id="g_c1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_enable_color="off" custom_padding="15px||||false|false" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"]

[committee number=1]
[question committee=1 number=1]Would Conference consider the adoption of inclusive wording in the AA GB Preamble specifically replacing “a fellowship of men and women” with “a fellowship of people” in light of AA’s Responsibility Pledge and the Third Tradition.

[/question]

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" global_colors_info="{}"][pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Conf-2022-General-Service-Conference-2022-Final-Report.pdf#page=74 name=Conf-2022-General-Service-Conference-2022-Final-Report.pdf name=Conf-2022-General-Service-Conference-2022-Final-Report.pdf]2022 Conference Decision (Committee 5, Question 1)[/pdf_link] Responsibility Pledge I am Responsible. When anyone, anywhere, reaches out for help, I want the hand of A.A. always to be there. And for that: I am responsible. [tradition number=3][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"]

[answer committee=1 question=1]

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=1 number=2]Would Conference discuss and recommend suggestions as to how our AA Groups could increase their 7th Tradition contributions so that our Fellowship is as financially robust as it can be for the future in terms of carrying its message to the still suffering alcoholic?[/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" global_colors_info="{}"]What Issue Does This Address? In recent years (since 2022/23) the Prudent Reserves across the Fellowship has come under pressure due to declining contributions and increasing costs. With societal moves towards a cashless society and the norm of “the pound in the pot” this is likely to continue unless the trend can be reversed. Whilst Gift Aid and Gratitude Week are good and worthy initiatives, they will not address the ongoing structural issues the Fellowship faces in terms of its ongoing funding needs from Groups down through structure to the General Service Board. It is therefore appropriate that this issue be addressed as soon as practicable. Has This Issue Been Discussed Before Within … The ongoing finances of the Fellowship have been discussed extensively in Conference and all through structure in recent years. From experience this is very much a “hot topic” across the Fellowship as a whole. [tradition number=7] [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=34 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]Concept XII - Warranty Two (The AA Structure Handbook for GB 2025/26)[/pdf_link] [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/3395-The-Pot.pdf name=The-Pot.pdf]The Pot… Where AA money and spirituality mix (AAGB pamphlet)[/pdf_link] [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=100 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]AA Money - Page 100 AA Structure Handbook for GB 2025/26[/pdf_link] How Will the Alcoholic Who Still Suffers Benefit ? By ensuring the hand of AA will aways be there.[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title="References" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" disabled_on="on|on|on" admin_label="Question 2 References" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" disabled="on" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

[pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/3180-Problems-Other-Than-Alcohol.pdf name=Problems-Other-Than-Alcohol.pdf]Problems Other Than Alcohol Pamphlet[/pdf_link]

[pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/3140-The-AA-Member-Medications-and-Other-Drugs.pdf name=3140-The-AA-Member-Medications-and-Other-Drugs.pdf]The AA Member - Medications & Other Drugs Pamphlet[/pdf_link]

[tradition number=3]

[tradition number=5]

Primary Purpose Card (meeting opening statements) – aka [pdf_link url=https://www.aa.org/sites/default/files/literature/assets/f-17_primarypurposestatementcard.pdf name=f-17_primarypurposestatementcard.pdf]The Blue Card[/pdf_link]

[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=1 question=2][/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 3" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=1 number=3]

Review revised Chapter 7 of the AA Service Handbook for Great Britain & Continental Europe – AA in Prisons.

[/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 3 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/2.-Background-Committee-1-Question-3-Revised-Chapter-7-Prisons-.pdf name=Background-Committee-1-Question-3-Revised-Chapter-7-Prisons-.pdf]Revised Chapter 7 Prisons[/pdf_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

[answer committee=1 question=3]

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 2" module_id="g_c2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][committee number=2] [question committee=2 number=1]

Would Conference share their experience, and consider the need to expand on the guidance provided in the Structure Handbook (page 119) with specific reference to children, minors and juveniles attending AA meetings?

How Will the Alcoholic Who Still Suffers Benefit …… Although groups are autonomous, by sharing their experience how we might accommodate parents/guardians and their children in meetings we will make our meetings more accessible to those with parental demands. By making it clearer how best the Fellowship can support children, minors and juveniles who may suffer from alcoholism we will be helping our members to be of maximum service to those so affected. [/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=119 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]AA Structure Handbook for GB 2025/26 - Page 119[/pdf_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title="References" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" disabled_on="on|on|on" admin_label="Question 1 References" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" disabled="on" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]Starting a Group [tradition number=1] [tradition number=3] [tradition number=4] [tradition number=10][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=2 question=1][/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=2 number=2] Would Conference discuss how the Conference process could be made more attractive, inclusive, and engaging for the Fellowship, so that all members and groups feel connected to, and part of, the way AA’s collective conscience is expressed? [/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]The General Service Conference is where AA’s collective conscience is expressed for our Fellowship as a whole. Concept One tells us that the final authority and responsibility rests with the groups. But some members/groups feel distant from the process, unsure how to connect with it. The aim is to help all members feel connected to our collective conscience. [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=30 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]Alcoholics Anonymous Great Britain & Continental Europe – Section 6. The Twelve Concepts[/pdf_link] [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=146 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]The General Service Conference[/pdf_link] General Service Conference Pamphlet Unable to Find[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title="References" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" disabled_on="on|on|on" admin_label="Question 2 References" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" disabled="on" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]Traditions [tradition number=1] [tradition number=2] [tradition number=3] [tradition number=4] [tradition number=5] [tradition number=6] [tradition number=8] [tradition number=10] [tradition number=11] [tradition number=12] Concepts 2. The General Service Conference of A.A. has become, for nearly every practical purpose, the active voice and the effective conscience of our whole society in its world affairs. 4. At all responsible levels, we ought to maintain a traditional “Right of Participation,” allowing a voting representation in reasonable proportion to the responsibility that each must discharge. The AA Group AAGB Primary Purpose Card [pdf_link url=https://www.aa.org/sites/default/files/literature/assets/f-17_primarypurposestatementcard.pdf name=f-17_primarypurposestatementcard.pdf]The Blue Card[/pdf_link] AA GB Website – Members section: About AA Meetings Open meetings are open to non-alcoholics too, such as family and friends supporting a loved one. Closed Meetings are limited to alcoholics and those who think or know they have a problem with drinking. AA GB Structure Handbook – Page 100 Group Meetings The Closed Meeting is intended for alcoholics and for those with an alcohol problem who have a desire to stop drinking. The Open Meeting is intended for alcoholics and non alcoholics e.g. family, friends and anyone interested in AA.[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=2 question=2][/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 3" module_id="g_c3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" collapsed="on" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][committee number=3] [question committee=3 number=1] What Issue Does This Address?

I came to the Fellowship as a 57-year-old man who thought they had they had lived their life with alcoholic loneliness my only future. Whilst I was welcomed on my arrival I found (very) few people coming to the Fellowship at a similar age to myself and who had similar shared experience, though 12 AA Service News Winter 2025 I did see specific support for younger people, people of colour, people with issues other than alcohol, gender diversity etc., etc. Whilst not feeling excluded I did feel my entry point diﬀerent from many people and in hindsight some additional support would have been welcomed.

In his excellent article in the AA Winter Service News (Winter 2024) Dr Kieran Moriaty wrote about alcohol and older people and described it as a “silent epidemic”. From personal experience as a Regional Health Liaison Oﬃcer I now see this firsthand in many of the service activities I undertake and it would be good to see our Fellowship rise more specifically to the challenge of this “silent epidemic”.

How Will the Alcoholic Who Still Suffers Benefit…. By making the Fellowship more accessible to older people we will make it easier for this growing segment of society to find the recovery they need and broaden the base and representation of our Fellowship in the future. [/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/3250-AA-For-the-Older-Alcoholic.pdf name=AA-For-the-Older-Alcoholic.pdf]“AA For The Older Alcoholic -Never To Late”.[/pdf_link] [open_new_link href=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/professionals/health/alcohol-problems-in-older-people]Alcohol and Older People - A Silent Epidemic - AA Service News (Winter 2024) - Dr Kieran Moriaty (NA Trustee)[/open_new_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

[answer committee=3 question=1]

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=3 number=2]

Would Conference consider and share their experience of how the Fellowship can improve its understanding and application of the 12 Concepts within Service?

[/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=32 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]Alcoholics Anonymous Great Britain & Continental Europe – Section 6.2 Twelve Concepts for Service[/pdf_link] [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=35 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]Alcoholics Anonymous Great Britain & Continental Europe – Section 6.3 Bill W.’s essays on The Twelve Concepts for World Service.[/pdf_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=3 question=2][/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 4" module_id="g_c4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][committee number=4] [question committee=4 number=1]Would the Fellowship discuss and share experience regarding how we might strengthen our practical application of Tradition One — “Our common welfare should come first; personal recovery depends upon AA unity” — so that we maintain safety and unity within AA, and avoid the need to use the term “safeguarding” in our literature? [/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]When applied practically, Tradition One leads us to create safe, welcoming environments, in which the still-suffering alcoholic can recover. In recent years, Conference discussion has given rise to literature being created by the Board and subsequently approved by Conference: it can be found in the Structure Handbook P115-123. The General Service Board (as a charity) is required by law to hold a Safeguarding Policy. Some members believe that the Fellowship itself should not be seen to adopt external policy frameworks, or use terms like ‘safeguarding’, as they may compromise AA’s Traditions. If groups use this question to discuss their practice of common welfare, and share with Conference their conclusions and suggestions, then we will strengthen the daily, practical application of Tradition One - in meetings, service structures, sponsorship, and Fellowship life - so that AA continues to offer a safe and unified environment in which our primary purpose (Tradition Five) can be fulfilled. [tradition number=1] [tradition number=5][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=4 question=1][/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=4 number=2]Would Conference share members experience of the safeguarding role at all levels of the service structure to inform a role description and sobriety length for the Service Handbook. [/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]Guidance would ensure those in Safeguarding roles are of maximum benefit/service in supporting the groups to adhere to healthy safeguarding practices in their meetings. This, in turn would enable the group to better carry the message to the still suffering alcoholic, protect the newcomer, members and the fellowship as a whole. [pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf#page=115 name=AA-Structure-Handbook-202526.pdf]AA Structure Handbook: Chapter 5 – The Group – Safeguarding and Personal Conduct.[/pdf_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title="References" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" disabled_on="on|on|on" admin_label="Question 2 References" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" disabled="on" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]Safeguarding section in [pdf_link url=http://aa-bristol.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/AA-Structure-Handbook-2024.pdf name=AA-Structure-Handbook-2024.pdf]Structure Handbook[/pdf_link] Group – P115 - 122 Annex C P196 [open_new_link href=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/about-aa/safeguarding class=]Website Document Library Safeguarding Policy[/open_new_link][/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

[answer committee=4 question=2]

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 5" module_id="g_c5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][committee number=5] [question committee=5 number=1]Would Conference consider the impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on Alcoholics Anonymous, and more specifically:
  1. How AI might assist the Fellowship in carrying out its primary purpose;
  2. What risks AI may pose to AA, especially regarding anonymity, data privacy and automation;
  3. Whether shared experience and/or guidance is needed to help ensure any use of AI remains in line with AA’s Traditions and spiritual principles.
[/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Artificial Intelligence is advancing rapidly and becoming an integral part of everyday life, already influencing how people seek help, connect with others, create content, and access information. These developments may also affect how members and newcomers encounter AA, as well as how service is carried out within the Fellowship.

For example, AI is increasingly used in online search, automated translation, content generation and interactive chat tools—areas that could help make AA’s message and services more widely accessible. At the same time, such technologies raise important concerns around anonymity, data privacy, and automation (i.e. no longer one alcoholic helping another).

It therefore seems timely for the Fellowship to consider AI’s potential impact, both positive and negative, and to decide whether shared experience or guidance is needed to ensure its use remains in line with AA’s Traditions and spiritual foundations.

[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=5 question=1][/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=5 number=2]

Would Conference share its experience on the importance of the Greeter role at our meetings, and make recommendations for inclusion in the Structure Handbook.

[/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Most group level roles are detailed in the Structure Handbook but this vitally important role has been overlooked.

[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=5 question=2][/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row admin_label="Committee 6" module_id="g_c6" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][committee number=6] [question committee=6 number=1]

Could Conference explore and recommend safe ways for AA members in early sobriety to re-engage socially in light of the increasing presence of alcohol-free beers, wines, and spirits on the market?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 1 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

An old AA saying is that when Daniel escaped the lion’s den with his head, he didn’t go back for his hat! However, AA newcomers are faced with the difficulties of returning to social situations at work and with friends.

The opinion of established AA members can differ on these issues. Some feel that recovering alcoholics can drink alcohol-free drinks whereas others are of the opinion that this is wrong. Some members remark that ‘non-alcoholic beer is for non-alcoholic people’.

AA Big Book references:
  • These allergic types can never safely use alcohol in any form at all [open_new_link href=https://www.aa.org/sites/default/files/2021-11/en_bigbook_foreworddoctorsopinion.pdf](‘The Doctor’s Opinion’, p. xxvi)[/open_new_link]
  • If tempted, we recoil from it (alcohol) as from a hot flame [open_new_link href=https://www.aa.org/sites/default/files/2021-11/en_bigbook_chapt6.pdf#page=13](‘Into Action’, p84)[/open_new_link]
[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_text admin_label="Answer 1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][answer committee=6 question=1][/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label="Question 2" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][question committee=6 number=2]

Review revised Chapter 8 of the AA Service Handbook for Great Britain & Continental Europe – Probation/Criminal Justice Social Work Services (CJSWS)

[/question][/et_pb_text][et_pb_toggle title="Background" icon_color="#666666" toggle_icon="||divi||400" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="24px" open_icon_color="#000000" open_toggle_icon="||divi||400" open_use_icon_font_size="on" open_icon_font_size="24px" admin_label="Question 2 Background" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#f4f4f4" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

[pdf_link url=https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/3.-Background-Committee-6-Question-2-Revised-Chapter-8-Probation.pdf name=Revised-Chapter-8-Probation.pdf]Revised Chapter 8 - Probation/Criminal Justice Social Work Services (CJSWS)[/pdf_link]
